radiologist: Dr. Priya Anand (Medicine); Dr. Steven Marsh (Cardiology over-read)
indication: Post-operative day #1; brief self-terminating atrial fibrillation runs noted on telemetry — obtain 12-lead to characterize the baseline rhythm between runs.
findings: Rate 74 bpm. Rhythm: NORMAL SINUS at the time of recording (obtained between the brief telemetry runs of atrial fibrillation). Uniform P waves; PR 158 ms; QRS 88 ms; QTc 430 ms. Normal axis. No ST-T abnormality. No LVH by voltage. No evidence of ischemia. Atrial fibrillation was NOT captured on this 12-lead (the runs were 15-20 seconds and self-terminated); see telemetry event documentation.
impression: NORMAL SINUS RHYTHM at the time of this tracing. No ischemic or structural abnormality evident. The brief paroxysms of atrial fibrillation are documented on telemetry rather than on this 12-lead.
comparison: Compared with the preoperative ECG (6/10): unchanged, both sinus.
